    <test_case id="4">
        <description>Test MERGE statement with WITH clause containing sub-WITH that references outer CTE</description>
        <sql>
-- Setup
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS target_table CASCADE;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS source_table CASCADE;
CREATE TABLE target_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, val TEXT);
CREATE TABLE source_table (id INT, val TEXT);
INSERT INTO target_table VALUES (1, 'old'), (2, 'old');
INSERT INTO source_table VALUES (1, 'new'), (3, 'new');

-- Execution: MERGE with CTE containing inner WITH
WITH cte_source AS (SELECT * FROM source_table)
MERGE INTO target_table t
USING (
    WITH inner_cte AS (SELECT * FROM cte_source)
    SELECT * FROM inner_cte
) s
ON t.id = s.id
WHEN MATCHED THEN
    UPDATE SET val = s.val
WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN
    INSERT VALUES (s.id, s.val);

-- Verify
SELECT * FROM target_table ORDER BY id;

-- Teardown
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS target_table CASCADE;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS source_table CASCADE;
        </sql>
    </test_case>
    <test_case id="5">
        <description>Test complex nested WITH clauses in SELECT with recursion detection across DML statements</description>
        <sql>
-- Setup
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS test_complex CASCADE;
CREATE TABLE test_complex (a INT, b INT);
INSERT INTO test_complex VALUES (1, 100), (2, 200);

-- Execution: Multiple levels of WITH in UPDATE referencing outer CTEs
WITH outer1 AS (SELECT 1 AS key),
     outer2 AS (SELECT key + 1 AS key FROM outer1)
UPDATE test_complex t
SET b = (
    WITH inner1 AS (SELECT key FROM outer1),
         inner2 AS (SELECT key FROM outer2)
    SELECT i1.key * 1000 + i2.key * 100
    FROM inner1 i1, inner2 i2
)
WHERE t.a = 1;

-- Verify
SELECT * FROM test_complex ORDER BY a;

-- Teardown
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS test_complex CASCADE;
        </sql>
